calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Hunter W. Sims III of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.4259, hunter.sims@sfcityatty.org) for defendant and moving party;
Christopher Kent of McCormick Barstow Sheppard Wayte & Carruth LLP (559.433.1300, christopher.kent@mccormickbarstow.com) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff and opposing party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OVERRULED, in part, and SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, in part. The untimely opposition is stricken. The Court notes that this is the third time that Plaintiff's opposition has been filed late and without leave of court.

The First Cause of Action for Breach of Contract and the Second Cause of Action for Breach of Contract and Breach of the Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing is overruled.

The Third Cause of Action for Unjust Enrichment is sustained without leave to amend. (See Lloyd v. Williams (1964) 227 Cal.App.2d 646, 649.)

The Fourth Cause of Action for Public Nuisance and the Fifth Cause of Action for Private Nuisance is overruled.

The Sixth Cause of Action for Inverse Condemnation is sustained without leave to amend. (County of Ventura v. Channel Islands Marina, Inc. (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 615.)

Defendant's argument that the First through Fifth causes of action should be sustained without leave to amend on account of the untimely Government Claim cannot be considered on demurrer as it was not raised in the prior demurrer. (Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 430.41(b).) Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2026 Continued To Jun-16-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2026 Continued To Mar-17-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Christopher Kent, Esq. for Plaintiff Herringbone Tavern
Hunter Sims, Esq. for Defendant City and County of San Francisco

Plaintiff offers proof to the court for the late filed opposition. Plaintiff acknowledges and apologizes and request leave to file opposition.
Defendant responds to Plaintiff statements.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised in the demurrer. Late-filed opposition is stricken. All amendments to be in bold font.
